events.name: Festa di San Giovanni Battista
events.intro: The day the village honors its protector, turning the streets into a canvas of devotion and color.
events.description: June 24th is the heartbeat of Riomaggiore's year. The celebration of San Giovanni Battista is not a show for visitors, but a deep-seated tradition. The village is decorated with 'infiorate'—intricate patterns made of flower petals and colored chalk on the pavement. A solemn procession winds through the narrow streets, carrying the statue of the saint down to the harbor, where the sea is blessed. It is a day of shared identity, ending with fireworks that reflect off the dark water of the marina.
events.giuliaComment: I love the morning of the 24th. The air smells of crushed petals and salt. I always watch the 'Madonnari' artists working on the pavement; there's a beautiful humility in creating something so complex that will be walked over by the end of the day. If you are here, find a spot on a staircase and just watch the procession pass. Don't try to follow it; let it move around you.
events.signature: A day when the village's history and faith become visible on the stone
events.timeOfDay: All day, procession in late afternoon
events.practicalInfo: The village is extremely crowded. Trains are frequent but packed. Arrive early if you want to see the flower art before the procession begins.

events.name: Madonna di Montenero Procession
events.intro: A vertical journey of faith, connecting the village to its sanctuary in the sky.
events.description: Twice a year—on Pentecost and September 8th—the village climbs. The pilgrimage to the Sanctuary of Montenero is a quiet, rhythmic event. Locals carry the image of the Madonna up the ancient mule tracks, past the vineyards that she is said to protect. At the top, high above the sea, a mass is held in the small piazza of the sanctuary. It is an event defined by breath, effort, and the wide silence of the hills.
events.giuliaComment: This is the 'real' Riomaggiore. It's not about the spectacle; it's about the climb. I notice how the older generation moves with a steady pace that puts younger hikers to shame. When you reach the sanctuary, the view of the coastline feels like a reward for the devotion. It's a very contemplative day.
events.signature: A pilgrimage that measures faith in footsteps and altitude
events.location: Trail from Piazza Vignaioli to the Sanctuary
events.practicalInfo: Requires a steady uphill walk. Wear comfortable shoes and bring water. The atmosphere is respectful and quiet; it is more of a shared walk than a festival.

events.name: The Grape Harvest (La Vendemmia)
events.intro: Not a festival, but a season of collective labor that defines the village's soul.
events.description: In September, the rhythm of Riomaggiore shifts to the terraces. The 'Vendemmia' is the most important event of the year, though it has no fixed schedule. It begins when the sun and the grapes decide. You will see the monorails (trenini) buzzing up and down the cliffs, laden with crates of Bosco, Albarola, and Vermentino grapes. The air in the upper village smells of fermenting must, and every conversation turns to the quality of the sugar and the timing of the rain.
events.giuliaComment: The Vendemmia is a time of high tension and great joy. I love the sound of the monorails—it's the soundtrack of our survival. You might find some paths closed or see farmers moving quickly with heavy loads; give them space. This is the work that keeps the village alive. If you're lucky, a local might offer you a taste of the fresh juice.
events.signature: The season when the hills yield their liquid gold
events.practicalInfo: This is a working period. Be mindful of farmers on narrow paths. Some hiking trails may have increased activity or temporary restrictions.

events.name: Rimazû Folk Festival
events.intro: A celebration of the local tongue, where the dialect of the rocks finds its voice in song.
events.description: The Rimazû Folk Festival is a rare moment where the specific culture of Riomaggiore takes center stage. Dedicated to the local dialect and traditional music, it brings together singers and storytellers in the village squares. It is an exploration of memory, preserving the words and melodies that have echoed through these valleys for centuries. It feels intimate, like a family gathering that the whole village is invited to.
events.giuliaComment: Even if you don't understand a word of the dialect, you can feel the emotion. It's a rough, salt-hewn language, and hearing it sung is very moving. I always notice the younger kids listening to the elders; it's how we keep our soul from being washed away by the tide of tourism.
events.signature: The voice of the village, preserved in melody and dialect
events.location: Piazza del Vignaiolo or the Castle
events.practicalInfo: A very local event. Seating is limited and usually fills up with residents. Standing at the edges is perfectly fine and often offers a better perspective.

events.name: Festa dell'Assunta
events.intro: The peak of summer, marked by a quiet devotion in the heart of the village.
events.description: August 15th, Ferragosto, is celebrated with particular intensity at the Oratory of Santa Maria Assunta. While the rest of Italy is at the beach, the Confraternity of the Assumption gathers for a solemn mass and a small procession. The simple stone oratory is filled with incense and the sound of ancient hymns. It is a moment of stillness in the busiest month of the year, a reminder of the village's spiritual foundations.
events.giuliaComment: I find this day very grounding. The heat outside is intense, but inside the oratory, it is cool and dark. I love the contrast between the holiday crowds on Via Colombo and the absolute focus of the people inside the church. It's a good day to practice 'scarpetta'—the art of moving slowly and noticing the small things.
events.signature: A cool refuge of faith in the heat of the summer
events.practicalInfo: The oratory is small; if you wish to attend mass, arrive early and dress respectfully (shoulders covered). The procession is short and stays within the central carruggi.

events.name: The Underwater Nativity
events.intro: A silent, luminous tribute to the sea that sustains and defines us.
events.description: On Christmas Eve, the harbor of Riomaggiore becomes a place of quiet wonder. Divers descend into the dark, cold water of the marina to place a nativity scene on the seabed. Torches illuminate the water from below, creating a ghostly, beautiful glow. On the shore, the village gathers with candles, singing carols that are carried away by the winter wind. It is a stark, beautiful event that connects the birth of Christ to the life of the fisherman.
events.giuliaComment: Winter in Riomaggiore is a secret that few visitors know. This event is my favorite. There is no noise, no fireworks—just the light in the water and the sound of the waves. It's incredibly moving. Wear your warmest coat and bring a thermos of tea. It's a night for standing close to others.
events.signature: A light in the winter dark, where the sea and the sacred meet
events.practicalInfo: It can be very cold and windy at the harbor. The rocks can be slippery in the dark; stay on the paved areas. Most restaurants are closed, so plan your dinner in advance.
